---
name: Replication / counter-analysis
about: Report a RUIN run on another corpus, or contest a specific scoring result
title: "[replication] "
labels: replication
---

## What did you run RUIN on?

- Journal / venue:
- Year range:
- Number of papers analysed:
- Repository or release link with the JSON output (one file per batch):

## Which LLM runtime and model did you use?

- Runtime (Claude Code, OpenAI Assistants, custom agent, human analyst, …):
- Model name and version:
- Framework specification version (see `analysis_version` in the JSON):

## Summary of findings

Two or three sentences. What did you observe — overall scores, flag rates,
notable spikes or deficits? Did your findings agree or disagree with what
similar metrics suggest for this corpus?

## (Optional) Contestation

If you believe a specific RUIN score is wrong, please include:

- The batch ID and paper ID
- The score(s) you dispute and what you believe is correct
- A short justification — ideally with reference to the Necessity Test,
  concept-complexity level, or specific flag conditions
- The provenance excerpt from the JSON, so the disagreement is anchored to
  evidence the framework can act on
